data.title: Cadence's 'stronger' Q1 results and outlook draw praise from analysts
data.description:
Shares of Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) dipped about 2% premarket on Tuesday despite first-quarter results and outlook seeing largely positive reactions from analysts.

Needham kept its Buy rating on Cadence's shares with a $400 price target.

"Cadence reported a usual beat-and-raise quarter, and raised the FY outlook largely on the organic and inorganic strength in 1H26. The company did not raise the 2H26 outlook by much at this point. However, behind the unchanged 2H26 outlook, we think the agentic AI momentum is quietly building, creating the condition for the company to meaningfully raise 2H26 outlook as the quarter progresses. Management reiterated strong confidence in strong license growth for base tools and incremental opportunity from new agentic tools based on the new subscription +consumption model," said analysts led by Charles Shi.

The analysts added that Cadence's management strongly hinted at pricing discipline that should enable a better value capture of agentic AI than all prior generations of AI tools. Shi and his team noted that while their estimates have moved up only moderately, their conviction is growing.

J.P. Morgan maintained its Overweight rating and $405 price target on the stock.

"Cadence’s Q1 results and FY26 outlook are arguably stronger than the headline EPS guide cut would suggest, given its record $8B backlog, organic raises in revenue/EPS/cash flow obscured by Hexagon-related dilution in the reported figures, a fully expanded agentic AI portfolio with monetization yet to be reflected in the numbers, and an explicit pricing power inflection," said analysts led by Harlan Sur.

In September 2025, Cadence said it was acquiring the design and engineering business of Hexagon, which includes its MSC Software business, for €2.7B.

The analysts added that the headline 2026 EPS guidance cut of $0.20 obscures a stronger underlying picture — stripping out the $0.28 of Hexagon dilution now baked into the 2026 outlook (Hexagon contributing $160M of revenue), the organic picture is one of incremental strength.

The analysts noted that 2026 represents a lighter renewal year than 2025, and historical patterns suggest these have been the strongest growth years given add-on activity (supportive of upward revisions through the year). The analysts added that on the agentic AI front, the portfolio is now fully built out, with two new Super Agents joining ChipStack to span the entire chip design flow, alongside the new Agent Stack framework, a Google/Gemini collaboration, and an expanded Nvidia partnership.

Sur and his team said that the company's management was explicit that the guidance does not assume "a sudden step-function in AI monetization," but with agents calling base tools at 10 to 100 times exploration per design block, the electronic design automation, or EDA, consumption ramp represents a structural multi-year upside lever that consensus has not yet fully calibrated.

"Equally important, mgmt called out for the first time that “the pricing environment has improved,” and separately noted that EDA’s share of customer R&D spend has expanded from approximately 7% historically to roughly 11% today, with further expansion expected as AI drives greater automation, both structural growth and margin tailwinds that, in our estimation, are absent fromcurrent consensus," the analysts added.

Related stock: Shares of Synopsys (SNPS) fell about 2% premarket on Tuesday.

data.title: Micron slides even as DA Davidson starts with Buy rating, Wall Street high price target
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) shares slid 2.5% in premarket trading on Tuesday even as investment firm DA Davidson initiated coverage on the memory maker with a Buy rating and a new Wall Street high price target. 

“We believe artificial intelligence is creating a longer-than-usual memory cycle as compute deployment and demand generation exist in a positive feedback loop, creating a structurally higher ceiling for memory pricing and demand,” analyst Gil Luria wrote in a note to clients. “The market is still framing the cycle through the lens of prior downturns, which appears to underestimate the demand environment, especially relative to the rest of the semi complex. Combined with Micron's node leadership and what we see as a long duration earnings power story, we see meaningful upside to shares.”

In addition to the Buy rating, Luria put a $1,000 price target on Micron.

Delving deeper, Luria said that the current memory cycle is not like prior ones, which saw capacity arrive in “lumpy increments,” and supply eventually outpaced demand. “In today's cycle, compute deployment and demand generation exist in a positive feedback loop where each additional deployment unlocks new use cases, creating incremental demand that didn't exist before the infrastructure was built,” Luria added. “Our view is supported by the signing of five-year SCAs that effectively lock in demand and offer visibility in a manner unlike past cycles while negotiating pricing on what we believe to be a quarterly or more frequent basis. We are not arguing that there isn't a cycle, just that the duration and extent of the cycle may not be priced in properly.”

Luria also noted that Micron has node leadership across dynamic random access memory, high-bandwidth memory and NAND. That advantage is likely to continue and improve Micron's cost position with each generation, allowing it to gain share in higher-margin products.

Lastly, in looking out to fiscal 2030, Micron could generate as much as $393B in revenue and as much as an adjusted $139 per share in earnings, Luria added.

data.title: Barclays trades lower despite higher Q1 earnings as impairments hit coverage ratio
data.description:
Barclays (BCS) was trading lower despite posting higher Q1 financial results on the back of a one-off charge and impairments that hit the bank's coverage ratio.

Shares were 2.47% lower at $22.52 before the market opened on Tuesday.

Basic earnings per ordinary share stood at £0.14, up from £0.13 in the first quarter of 2025. Total income increased 6% to £8.16B from £7.71B.

Attributable profit rose 4% to £1.93B from £1.86B.

Credit impairment charges rose to £823M from £643M. The increase was primarily driven by a single name charge of £228M in investment banking.

"Uncertainty persists and this is reflected in a net £20M increase in related management adjustments. As a result, total coverage ratio increased to 1.3% (December 2025: 1.2%)," said the British lender in its earnings release.

In March, Barclays CEO CS Venkatakrishnan had said he's "obviously disappointed" that the bank was involved with Market Financial Solutions, a property lender that collapsed earlier this year. However, the anticipated impairment for MFS is "materially lower" than Barclays' £500M ($668M) exposure to the firm.

Return on average tangible shareholders' equity stood at 13.5%, down from the year-ago 14.0%.

Common equity tier 1 ratio fell slightly to 14.1% as of March 31, 2026, from 14.3% as of December 31, 2025. 

The company announced the initiation of a new up to £500M share buyback program.

data.title: TFI International lands a bull rating from BofA after earnings
data.description:
Bank of America upgraded TFI International (TFII) to a Buy rating after having it set at Neutral. The firm sees favorable exposure to supply-side and industrial demand catalysts for the North American transportation and logistics leader, as well as progress on productivity/serviceinitiatives and an attractive valuation vs. peers. 

"We see EPS rebounding off 2025 trough levels in 2026, led by logistics, truckload, and U.S. less-than-truckload," highlighted analyst Ken Hoexter. He noted that TFII has historically been subject to a lower multiple vs. LTL peers given its diverse exposure across categories.

Hoexter and his team assigned a price objective of $161.00 on TFI (TFII). The multiple was moved further above the stock's 11X to 17X range as earnings moved past the trough, and BofA sees potential for strong cash generation, idiosyncratic U.S. LTL operational improvement, and upside from supportive specialty/flatbed rate dynamics. 

Shares of TFI International (TFII) were up 4.8% in premarket trading on Tuesday as investors sized up the Quebec-based company's double beat with its Q1 earnings report.

data.title: Incyte taps Zimmer Biomet's Suketu Upadhyay as CFO
data.description:
• Incyte (INCY) on Tuesday said it has hired Suketu Upadhyay as its CFO, effective May 4, 2026.
  • Upadhyay most recently served as CFO at Zimmer Biomet (ZBH). He also previously held a senior finance leadership role at Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) and was CFO at Endo International.
  • “Suky will oversee all aspects of our finance function. He will guide our capital allocation approach and apply effective financial stewardship in support of value creation for our shareholders. He is known and respected for his detail‑oriented approach, objective decision‑making, and disciplined investment mindset. Suky will contribute both strategically and operationally as a core member of Incyte’s Executive Leadership Team,” said Bill Meury, Chief Executive Officer, Incyte.
  • Shares were down -2% in premarket trading. The company also reported earnings on Tuesday, with Q1 adjusted EPS of $1.81, beating estimates by $0.47. Revenue of $1.27B (+21.0% Y/Y) beat estimates by $50M.

data.title: Central Bancompany GAAP EPS of $0.46 beats by $0.03, revenue of $270.56M in-line
data.description:
• Central Bancompany, Inc. press release (CBC): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.46 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $270.56M (+10.4% Y/Y) in-line.
  • GAAP net interest income of $208.6 million, reflecting a GAAP net interest margin (“NIM”) of 4.32% compared to 4.38% in the prior quarter and 4.19% in the prior year quarter
  • Average total loans held for investment of $11.5 billion, quarterly increase of $0.1 billion, or 1.2% growth from the prior quarter
  • Average total deposits of $15.5 billion, seasonally higher from last quarter and an increase of $0.8 billion or 5.2% from prior year quarter

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: S&P Global tops Q1 estimates; updates FY26 revenue growth
ON Semiconductor, Geely expand pact around SiC and 900V platforms
data.description:
• ON Semiconductor (ON) said on Tuesday it expanded a pact with Geely Auto (GELFY) for silicon carbide power technology and higher-voltage EV architectures.
  • The chipmaker's latest silicon carbide power technology will be integrated across vehicles built on Geely’s SEA-S platform to drive faster, more efficient EV development. The technology supports next-generation 900V EV architectures, enabling faster charging, longer driving range, and more consistent vehicle performance.
  • “Electrification is entering a new phase where system efficiency and scalability are paramount,” said Hassane El-Khoury, President and CEO of onsemi. “Our expanded collaboration with Geely demonstrates how deep engineering alignment and aligned technology roadmaps can accelerate the transition to high-voltage architectures."
